findings.issue: No config versioning or migration mechanism
findings.description: The config module lacks any version tracking or migration logic. When the config schema changes in future releases, there's no way to detect old configs, warn users, or migrate deprecated fields. New fields with #[serde(default)] silently get default values, but old deprecated fields would just be ignored by serde without warning. This could lead to config confusion when users upgrade.
findings.impact: Users upgrading to new versions may have stale config files with deprecated settings that silently disappear, or new required settings that get default values without notification. This can cause unexpected behavior changes after upgrades.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a config_version field to Config struct. Implement migration logic in Config::load to detect version mismatches and transform old configs to new schema, logging warnings about deprecated/added fields. Consider a separate ConfigMigration module for version-specific transformations.

findings.issue: Silent fallback to defaults on config load failure
findings.description: In GuiManager::update, Config::load_sync().unwrap_or_default() silently falls back to defaults if config loading fails, with no user notification. A corrupted config file or parse error would be silently masked, and users would get unexpected default settings without knowing their config was ignored.
findings.impact: Users with corrupted configs may not notice that their settings were replaced with defaults. Important settings like save_directory or hotkeys could change unexpectedly without notification.
findings.suggestedFix: Use Config::load_sync() with proper error handling, and show a toast/error notification to the user if loading fails. Consider logging the specific error to help users diagnose config issues.

findings.issue: MicVolume type inconsistency with other volume fields
findings.description: mic_volume is u16 with clamp to max 100, while system_volume and master_volume are u8 with clamp to max 200. The u16 type allows values up to 65535 theoretically, but the clamp prevents going above 100. This inconsistency in types and ranges is confusing and could indicate an oversight.
findings.impact: The inconsistent types and ranges may confuse developers and users. The u16 type for mic_volume is unnecessary since it's capped at 100, which fits in u8. UI code needs to handle different ranges for different volumes.
findings.suggestedFix: Standardize all volume fields to the same type (u8) and range (0-100 or 0-200). If mic_volume intentionally has a different max (100 vs 200), document this in the field comment and consider using u8 since 100 fits.

findings.description: The save_directory is just a String with no validation in the validate() method. It could contain invalid paths, non-existent directories, paths with illegal characters for Windows, relative paths, or UNC paths that may not work. The validation function only clamps numeric values.
findings.impact: Users could set invalid save_directory values that cause clip saving to fail later in the output module. The error would occur at save time rather than at config load/validation time, making debugging harder.
findings.suggestedFix: Add validation in Config::validate() to check: path is not empty, contains valid Windows path characters, exists or parent exists for creation, absolute path preferred. Warn on invalid paths and optionally set a safe fallback.

findings.description: HotkeyConfig does not derive PartialEq, unlike AudioConfig which does. This requires requires_hotkey_reregister() to manually compare string fields. The inconsistency makes comparison logic scattered and could lead to bugs if new hotkey fields are added without updating the comparison.
findings.impact: Adding new hotkey fields would require manual update to requires_hotkey_reregister(). The pattern is inconsistent with other structs that derive PartialEq for comparison.
findings.suggestedFix: Add #[derive(PartialEq)] to HotkeyConfig and simplify requires_hotkey_reregister() to self.hotkeys != other.hotkeys. This makes field additions safer and code cleaner.

findings.issue: Quality_value accepted even when rate_control is not Cq
findings.description: When rate_control is VBR or CBR, quality_value is still allowed but will be ignored by the encoder. The validation only sets a default quality_value when rate_control is Cq but doesn't warn or clear quality_value for other rate control modes.
findings.impact: Users might set quality_value expecting it to work, but it will be silently ignored for VBR/CBR modes. This could cause confusion when users think they're controlling quality but the value isn't used.
findings.suggestedFix: In validate(), if rate_control is not Cq and quality_value is set, either: (a) warn that quality_value will be ignored, or (b) clear it to None with a warning. Document clearly which rate control modes use quality_value.

findings.issue: Resolution/use_native_resolution inconsistency only logged, not fixed
findings.description: When use_native_resolution=false but resolution=Native, validate() logs a warning and sets use_native_resolution to true. When use_native_resolution=true with a specific resolution, it only logs a warning that resolution will be ignored. The resolution field isn't actually changed in the latter case.
findings.impact: Users might have confusing config state where resolution=1080p but use_native_resolution=true, leading to the 1080p setting being ignored. The config file retains the inconsistent value.
findings.suggestedFix: Either normalize both cases consistently: (a) set resolution=Native when use_native_resolution=true, or (b) set use_native_resolution=false when resolution!=Native. Ensure the saved config reflects the actual effective state.

findings.description: gpu_index has no validation in validate(). A user could set an invalid GPU index (e.g., 5 when they only have GPU 0-2) which will fail later during pipeline initialization when trying to use that GPU for capture/encode.
findings.impact: Invalid gpu_index values cause pipeline startup failures rather than early config validation errors. The failure is delayed and harder to attribute to the config setting.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider adding validation to check if gpu_index is plausible (range 0-3 or similar). Alternatively, document that gpu_index validation happens at runtime since actual GPU count varies by system.

findings.issue: Audio balance lacks documentation of scale meaning
findings.description: Audio balance is clamped to -100 to 100 but there's no documentation explaining what this scale means (e.g., -100 = full left, 100 = full right). The validation just clamps without providing context.
findings.impact: Users and developers may be unclear on what balance values mean. The scale -100 to 100 is intuitive but could benefit from explicit documentation.
findings.suggestedFix: Add documentation comment to the balance field explaining: -100 = full left channel, 0 = centered, 100 = full right. Consider adding this to the struct field comment in types.rs.

findings.description: The final conversion `total_bytes.min(usize::MAX as u64) as usize` could truncate values on 32-bit systems where usize::MAX is ~4GB. Large replay durations or bitrates could overflow usize. While this is unlikely for typical configs, the code assumes 64-bit usize.
findings.impact: On 32-bit systems (unlikely for this Windows desktop app but possible), large config values could cause truncated byte estimates. The saturating math earlier prevents u64 overflow, but usize truncation could occur.
findings.suggestedFix: Either: (a) document that this code assumes 64-bit platform (app targets Windows 10+ which is 64-bit), or (b) use checked cast with error handling. Since the app is Windows desktop focused, this is likely acceptable.

findings.issue: Edge case: root path as config_file
findings.description: The code `config_path.parent().context("Config path has no parent directory")` handles missing parent, but a root path like `C:\` as config_file would have parent `C:\` (same), which is unusual but handled. The edge case of truly rootless paths is covered by the context error.
findings.impact: Minor edge case handling. Users unlikely to set root path as config location. The error handling is adequate.
findings.suggestedFix: Current implementation is adequate. The error message "Config path has no parent directory" is clear. No change needed.

findings.issue: Good fallback chain for default save directory
findings.description: The default save_directory uses a sensible fallback chain: dirs::video_dir() first, then USERPROFILE environment variable, then hardcoded C:\Videos fallback. This handles edge cases well where standard dirs may be unavailable.
findings.impact: Positive pattern - robust default path resolution that handles missing user profile or video directory.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. This is a good pattern worth noting for other path defaults.

findings.issue: Auto-creates default config when file missing
findings.description: When config file doesn't exist, load_with_dirs creates defaults, validates, writes to disk, and returns. This ensures users always have a valid config file to edit. The pattern is user-friendly.
findings.impact: Positive UX pattern - users get a working config immediately without manual creation.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. This pattern is good for desktop apps.

findings.issue: Good logging pattern for config corrections
findings.description: All validation corrections use tracing::warn with descriptive messages explaining what was clamped and why. This helps users debug unexpected config behavior by checking logs.
findings.impact: Positive pattern - transparent validation with clear logging.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. The logging is helpful for debugging.

findings.issue: Hotkey validation delegates to separate module
findings.description: Hotkey validation is properly delegated to hotkey_parse module, which validates parseability and modifier requirements. The validation warns on invalid hotkeys rather than clearing them, allowing the user to fix.
findings.impact: Good modular design - hotkey parsing logic is centralized and reusable. Warning-only approach lets users see errors and fix config manually.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. This is a good pattern.

findings.issue: Default trait implementations split into separate files
findings.description: Default implementations for config structs are split into separate *_traits.rs files (generalconfig_traits.rs, videoconfig_traits.rs, etc.). This keeps types.rs focused on type definitions and reduces file size.
findings.impact: Good code organization - separates type definitions from trait implementations.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. The SplitRS-generated structure is clean.

findings.issue: Good comparison pattern for detecting restart requirements
findings.description: The methods provides clear comparison logic for which config changes require pipeline restart or hotkey re-registration. This is important for hot config updates without full app restart.
findings.impact: Positive pattern - explicit comparison makes it clear which settings are pipeline-level vs runtime adjustable.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. The pattern is correct and documented with tests.

findings.issue: Saturating arithmetic prevents overflow in calculations
findings.description: The memory estimation calculations use saturating_mul, saturating_add, and checked_div to prevent overflow. This handles extreme config values gracefully.
findings.impact: Positive pattern - robust arithmetic for user-controlled values that could be large.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. The overflow protection is appropriate.

findings.issue: Comprehensive unit tests for validation clamping
findings.description: functions.rs includes thorough tests for validation: framerate clamping, quality_value bounds, CQ default quality, memory limit bounds, etc. Tests verify validation behavior for edge cases.
findings.impact: Positive pattern - validation logic is well tested.
findings.suggestedFix: No change needed. Test coverage is good.

strengths: Good use of #[serde(default)] with explicit default functions for all fields
Saturating arithmetic in memory calculations prevents overflow
Comprehensive unit test coverage for validation logic
Auto-creation of default config when file missing
Clear comparison methods for restart requirements
weaknesses: No config versioning/migration mechanism for schema changes
GUI code silently falls back to defaults on load failure
Volume field types inconsistent (u16 vs u8, different max values)
Quality_value accepted but ignored for non-CQ rate control
recommendations: Add config_version field and migration logic for future schema changes
Add save_directory path validation in validate()
Handle config load errors in GUI with user notification
Standardize volume field types and ranges
Warn or clear quality_value for non-CQ rate control modes
Add documentation for audio balance scale meaning
